<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Summoner's Precaution](/Spells.aspx?ID=1011)
<actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Contingency" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=385" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 135

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** 10 minutes ([material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284))

 

**Requirements** You have an eidolon.
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** until your next daily preparations
</row>

</column>

---

You create a buffer in the link between yourself and your eidolon in order to prevent you from falling alongside your bonded ally. You gain the following reaction; after using it, the spell ends.

 **Sever Conduit** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32)) **Trigger** Your eidolon takes damage that would bring you to 0 Hit Points and comes from an effect other than a [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=40) effect; **Effect** You quickly shut the buffer in your link with your eidolon, causing your bonded ally to wink out of existence before you can be laid low. Your eidolon unmanifests, and you can't Manifest your Eidolon for 1 minute. In exchange, you don't take the triggering damage, though your eidolon still suffers any other adverse effects that accompanied the damage.
name: Summoner's Precaution

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Summoner's Visage](/Spells.aspx?ID=1012)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Polymorph" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=127" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 135

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** your  eidolon 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You transform your eidolon into your identical twin. Assuming you are a humanoid, the eidolon gains the [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=91) trait in addition to its other traits while it's in this form, as well as any other trait related to your form (such as [elf](/Traits.aspx?ID=58) or [gnome](/Traits.aspx?ID=80)). Your eidolon gains a +4 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) you and can add its level to such checks even if it's untrained.

 Although your eidolon looks like you, it doesn't gain any new abilities, and its statistics don't change in any way—the transformation is purely cosmetic. If this transformation reduces your eidolon's size, the eidolon loses any other effects of its larger size, such as any increase to [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192). Your eidolon can still use gear only if it has the [eidolon](/Traits.aspx?ID=386) trait, which allows eidolons to use it. Any such eidolon items the eidolon was wearing change size and shape, if necessary, and its effects remain active.

 You can Dismiss the spell.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The duration increases to 1 hour.
name: Summoner's Visage

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Synchronize](/Spells.aspx?ID=1013)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 135

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** up to 5 willing creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 24 hours
</row>

</column>

---

You harmlessly place your unique magic sigil, which is about 1 square inch in size, on your targets. When you Cast the Spell, you set the duration by choosing a time at which point the sigil flashes dimly three times. After that point, the spell ends. Even though spell durations aren't normally exact, the effects of _synchronize_ are precise to the second. The timer is based on the place where the spell was cast, so entering a plane or area where time flows differently changes how the time elapses.

---
**Heightened (2nd)** The spell can target up to 20 willing creatures.
name: Synchronize

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Tame](/Spells.aspx?ID=1014)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 135

**Traditions**
[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 10 feet

**Target** 1 non-hostile domesticated  animal 
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Will

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

As you make comforting sounds and gestures, you approach the target in a friendly manner combining caution and confidence. You improve the target's attitude to you by one step ([unfriendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=40) to neutral, neutral to [friendly](/Conditions.aspx?ID=18), friendly to [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21)) for the duration of the spell unless it succeeds at a Will save. Afterward, the target is temporarily immune for 1 day.

 This spell works on only domesticated animals; for example, you could use it on guard dogs or stray dogs, but not feral dogs or wolves. If the socialization of the animal is in question, the decision is up to the GM.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** The duration becomes 10 minutes.

**Heightened (6th)** The duration becomes 1 hour.
name: Tame

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 135 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 100 feet **Duration** until the end of your next turn --- You channel the forces of undeath to briefly call forth an amalgam of the vengeful dead slain by your enemies and allies alike. This amalgam manifests as a large tornado of insubstantial, howling faces. It occupies the space of a Huge creature and has a Speed of 60 feet. **Arrive** ([negative](/Traits.aspx?ID=118)) _Vortex of Death_ Each enemy creature within a 60-foot emanation must attempt a Fortitude save with the following effects.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected
**Success** The creature is [drained 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10).
**Failure** The creature is drained 2.
**Critical Failure** The creature is drained 3. **Depart** ([emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106)) _Howl of Eternity_ The vengeful dead lets out an anguished scream. All your enemies within a 100-foot emanation must attempt Will saves with the following effects.
**Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Failure** The creature is frightened 3.
**Critical Failure** The creature is frightened 3. It's also [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) for 1 round or until it's no longer frightened, whichever comes first.
name: Tempest of Shades

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 5" pfs="Standard">
[Temporary Glyph](/Spells.aspx?ID=1016)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 136

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

**Spell Lists**
[Elemental](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=5)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** a 5-foot square

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You temporarily bind a hostile spell into a symbol. While Casting this Spell, you also Cast a Spell of a lower spell level to store in the glyph. The stored spell must take 2 actions or fewer to Cast, have a hostile effect, and target one creature or have an area. Any creature that enters _temporary glyph's_ area activates the glyph, releasing the harmful spell within. You can set a password for the glyph. Speaking it when entering the spell's area prevents the glyph from triggering. You can also set a more specific [trigger](/Rules.aspx?ID=301) to limit which types of creatures set off the glyph.

 Once a spell is stored in the glyph, the glyph gains all the traits of that spell. If the stored spell can target one or more creatures, it targets the creature that set off the glyph. If it has an area, that area is centered on the creature that set off the glyph. _Temporary glyph's_ duration ends when the glyph is triggered. The glyph is a magical trap, using your spell DC for both the Perception check to notice it and the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to disable it; both checks require the creature attempting them to be trained in order to succeed.

 You can Dismiss _temporary glyph_.
name: Temporary Glyph

<title level="1" right="Spell 1" pfs="Standard">
[Thicket of Knives](/Spells.aspx?ID=1017)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Visual" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=163" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 136

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create numerous phantom copies of your weapon arm, hiding your true movements and rendering your attacks unpredictable. You gain a +2 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks. If you're untrained in Deception, you can use the [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) action anyway, and add your level as your proficiency bonus despite being untrained.
name: Thicket of Knives

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Thundering Dominance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1018)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 136

**Traditions**
[Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** your  companion  or  eidolon 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You shroud the target in a powerful predatory aura and amplify its vocalizations. It gains a +1 status bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks. Once during this spell's duration, it can take the Thundering Roar action.

 **Thundering Roar** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([auditory](/Traits.aspx?ID=16), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=60), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=68), [flourish](/Traits.aspx?ID=73), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106), [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147)) The target unleashes a powerful vocalization. Each enemy in a 10- foot emanation takes 4d8 sonic damage with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Will save against your spell DC. A creature that fails its Will save is also [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).

---
**Heightened (+2)** The status bonus to Intimidation checks increases by 1 (to a maximum of a +4 status bonus for an 8th-level spell), and the damage from the roar increases by 2d8.
name: Thundering Dominance

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Time Jump](/Spells.aspx?ID=1019)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 136

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Dachzerul](/Deities.aspx?ID=595)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You leap forward a few seconds in time, appearing across the battlefield in the blink of an eye. You gain 2 actions, each of which must be used to [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), Stand, Step, or Stride. If you have an appropriate Speed, you can add [Burrow](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climb](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) to this list.

 While you take these actions, time pauses. All other creatures are completely unaware of your actions, can't speak, and can't use any actions that would be triggered by your movement. While you're taking these actions, you can't take any other actions, including any that would be triggered by the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) actions. Once the actions are complete, time starts again, and to onlookers, you seem to have suddenly teleported across the distance you traveled. Leaping forward through time is disorienting, so if you use _time jump_ again within 1 minute of using the spell, you become [stupefied 4](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) for 1 minute.
name: Time Jump

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Timely Tutor](/Spells.aspx?ID=1020)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 137

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** your  eidolon  or  familiar 
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You serve as an astral connection between your eidolon or familiar and the Akashic Record—a demiplane consisting of a comprehensive psychic library—then turn to the creature for advice. If you cast this spell on your familiar, your familiar adds your spellcasting ability modifier on checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with the [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) skill of your choice, much like it does for [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15). Your familiar must have the [speech](/Familiars.aspx?ID=12) familiar ability in order to share any information it learns with you. If you cast this spell on your eidolon, it instead becomes trained in the Lore skill.

 If you lose physical contact with the target, its connection to the Akashic Record is severed and _timely tutor_ immediately ends.
name: Timely Tutor

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 137 **Traditions** [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 foe, and either yourself or 1 ally **Saving Throw** Fortitude --- You prove that slow and steady doesn't always win the race by turning a foe into a proverbial tortoise and yourself or an ally into a hare, siphoning some of the foe's speed and granting it to the other target. The effects depend on the foe's Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The targets are unaffected.
**Success** The foe is [slowed 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=35) for 1 round, and the other target is unaffected.
**Failure** The foe is slowed 1 for 3 rounds and the other target is [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) for 1 round. It can use the extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.
**Critical Failure** The foe is slowed 1 for 3 minutes and the other target is quickened for 1 minute. It can use the extra action to Step, Stride, or Strike.
name: Tortoise and the Hare

<title level="1" right="Spell 2" pfs="Standard">
[Umbral Extraction](/Spells.aspx?ID=1022)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 137

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Irez](/Deities.aspx?ID=472), [Nephthys](/Deities.aspx?ID=637)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 3 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

You prepare to steal spells from your foes, ripping them from the foe's shadow to stow them in an ephemeral pocket in the [Shadow Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=11). You gain a temporary spell slot, which you can fill only by stealing a spell from an enemy spellcaster. During _umbral extraction's_ duration, you can use the [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) action to attempt to take one of the foe's prepared spells or unused spontaneous spell slots instead of an item. You can also make one attempt to Steal as part of Casting _umbral extraction_. If you succeed at your check to Steal a spell, you deal 1d4 mental damage to the target per level of the spell stolen due to the psychological toll of the spiritual emptiness created by wrenching the spell away, and you place the stolen spell in your temporary spell slot. It gains the [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) trait if cast from this temporary slot.

 Determine the stolen spell at random from the target's spells that are 1 level lower than _umbral extraction_, or from spells of the same level if you got a critical success. If the target has no spell slots of that level, use the highest level below that in which the target does have a spell slot. (If the target has no eligible spells, you get nothing, but you can continue to attempt to Steal spells for the remaining duration.) If the target is a prepared caster, you randomly steal one of their prepared spells. If the target is a spontaneous caster, you instead steal an unexpended spell slot of the appropriate level and determine the spell at random from all the target's known spells at that level. You can't steal a target's innate spells, focus spells, or cantrips. You know what the spell is as soon as you steal it, and your temporary spell slot becomes a spell slot of the same level as the spell you stole.

 As normal, you can Steal only from a target within your reach. Once you've placed a spell in your temporary spell slot, you can't attempt to Steal more spells. If you succeed at your [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check, you must take the spell you stole. Even if you're a spontaneous caster, you can use the temporary spell slot only to cast the stolen spell and you can cast only the stolen spell from your temporary spell slot. When _umbral extraction_ ends, you lose the temporary spell slot and any spell still stored in it. When you cast _umbral extraction_, any previous casting of _umbral extraction_ currently affecting you ends.
name: Umbral Extraction

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Umbral Graft](/Spells.aspx?ID=1023)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 137

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 3 rounds
</row>

</column>

---

You reflect a spell into a shadowy version of itself, in the process depleting the power of the initial spell. During _umbral graft's_ duration, you can use the [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) action to attempt to abscond with spells affecting a foe instead of stealing an item. You can also make one attempt to Steal as part of Casting _umbral graft_. If you succeed at your check to Steal a spell, randomly determine a spell affecting the foe that has a target entry. That spell ceases  to affect the target, and you can either cause the spell to affect you or store it in your shadow. If your attempt to Steal was a critical success, you automatically steal the spell if its level is equal to or lower than _umbral graft_. If it was a success, you automatically steal it if its level is lower than _umbral graft_. If you succeeded, but the spell's level was too high to steal automatically, you must attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check to see if you steal the spell. You know what the spell is as soon as you steal it. If you store it, you can transfer it to a willing creature as a single action that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. The spell lasts for its remaining duration, to a maximum of 1 minute, and any time you keep it stored subtracts from its duration normally. The spell gains the [shadow](/Traits.aspx?ID=143) trait.

 You can't steal constant innate spells that are always active on a creature, such as a [choral angel's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=14) constant [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340), or innate spells that have special restrictions that remove their ability to target other creatures, such as an [imp's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=109) [_invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=164). At the GM's discretion, you might not be able to steal innate spells with other unusual modifications to the base spell.

 As normal, you can Steal only from a target within your reach. Once you've stolen a spell, you can't attempt to Steal more spells, nor can you choose not to take a spell if you succeed at your [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check. When you cast _umbral graft_, any spells you stole with a previous _umbral graft_ end even if their duration hasn't elapsed. If you're affected by both _umbral graft_ and [_umbral extraction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1022), you make the choice whether to steal a spell slot, an active spell, or an item each time you Steal.
name: Umbral Graft

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Unexpected Transposition](/Spells.aspx?ID=1024)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 138

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

**Trigger** You are targeted with an enemy's Strike.

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** 1 creature other than the triggering enemy
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Will

</row>

</column>

---

When attacked, you attempt to quickly swap your own position with that of another creature. A creature that is unwilling to swap places with you must attempt a Will save. Willing creatures automatically fail. If you successfully switch places with the target, the triggering attack is resolved against that creature as if it had been the original target of the attack. After the swap, you and the target are both temporarily immune to _unexpected transposition_ spells for 1 minute. You automatically switch places if the target is willing. If it's unwilling, it can attempt a Will save. Neither of you teleports if the target succeeds at its save.
name: Unexpected Transposition

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 138 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** varies --- You alter a creature's shadow, transforming it into a terrifying monster out to devour the creature. The creature must attempt a Will save. A creature that has the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) condition from _unspeakable shadow_ must spend at least one of its actions each turn to either attack its shadow (making a Strike ineffectually) or flee from its shadow (using one action to move away as though it had the [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=17) condition). **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target is frightened 2.
**Failure** The target is frightened 3. It can't reduce its frightened value below 1 for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** The target is so afraid, it might instantly die. It must succeed at a Fortitude save or die; this saving throw has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=93) trait. If it succeeds at its save, the target is frightened 4 and can't reduce its frightened value below 1 for 1 minute.
name: Unspeakable Shadow

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Variable Gravity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1026)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 138

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Telastmar](/Deities.aspx?ID=549)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 5 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You can adjust how strongly gravity grips you, changing between low gravity, normal gravity, and high gravity with ease. Choose one type of gravity when you cast the spell. While the spell lasts, you can change the type of gravity with a single action, which has the concentrate trait. The spell doesn't alter your physical appearance, so it's impossible to what type of gravity you're subjected to by casual observation.

- **Low Gravity** You can jump double the normal distance when you [Long Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=37) or [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81), though still to a maximum of your Speed. When you [High Jump](/Actions.aspx?ID=36), you can use the calculation for a Long Jump but don't double the distance. Rather than the normal calculation for falling damage, when you fall, you take no damage for the first 10 feet and then take bludgeoning damage equal to a quarter of the remaining distance you fell. Abilities that adjust how much falling damage you take still apply.
- **High Gravity** You become [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12). You gain a +2 status bonus to your saving throws and DCs to resist [forced movement](/Rules.aspx?ID=451) and attempts to knock you [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31). You reduce the distance forced movement moves you by half or 10 feet, whichever is more. You can only jump half as high and far. Rather than the normal calculation for falling damage, you take bludgeoning damage equal to the distance you fall. Abilities that adjust how much falling damage you take still apply.

---
**Heightened (6th)** The duration increases to 1 hour.
name: Variable Gravity

<title level="1" right="Spell 9" pfs="Standard">
[Voracious Gestalt](/Spells.aspx?ID=1027)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Aura" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=206" />
<trait label="Death" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=40" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 139

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 30-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You create a powerful spirit entity that grows and builds strength by consuming the spirits of foes you both slay. When you Cast the Spell, the gestalt deals 14d6 negative damage to all living creatures of your choice in the area, with a basic Fortitude save. Creatures you choose that end their turns in the area take 6d6 damage, with a basic Fortitude save.

 At the end of each of your turns, if you killed a living creature during that turn or if the gestalt's damage killed a creature since the end of your prior turn, you add the slain creature's soul to the gestalt. The emanation's radius increases by 20 feet and the damage dealt by the aura increases by 1d6. (Note that this increase only happens once, even if you killed multiple living creatures that turn.) As normal for determining threat level, the GM determines if a creature is of significant enough threat to empower the gestalt; in general, a creature several levels below you rarely poses a significant threat.

---
**Heightened (10th)** The initial damage increases to 16d6 and the aura's damage starts at 7d6.
name: Voracious Gestalt

<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Wall of Water](/Spells.aspx?ID=1028)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 139

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Raising your hand in the air, you call a massive wave onto the battlefield. The wall stays upright in a straight line that is 60 feet long, 10 feet high, and 5 feet thick.

 Any non-magical fires within the _wall of water's_ area that are its size or smaller are put out instantly. It also attempts to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) any magical fires in its area. Both of these effects happen when the spell is cast and whenever a fire enters the wall's area. If the wall fails to counteract a given fire, it can't counteract that fire for the duration of the spell.

 Bludgeoning or slashing projectiles can't pass through the wall, and piercing projectiles have their range  increments halved if they pass through the wall. A creature traversing the wall of water needs to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) through. The rules of [aquatic combat](/Rules.aspx?ID=467) apply to creatures traversing the _wall of water_, targeting creatures within the wall, or passing through the wall. For instance, a bludgeoning or slashing melee attack targeting a creature in the wall would take a –2 circumstance penalty.
name: Wall of Water

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 140 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** touch **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Will --- Regret eats away at the target's mind, punishing it for its violence until it ceases its hostility. The target must attempt a Will save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** At the end of its next turn, the target takes 1d8 damage per different creature it intentionally damaged on that turn (1d8 if it damaged one creature, 2d8 if it damaged two different creatures, and so on). The maximum is 4d8 if it damaged 4 different creatures.
**Failure** As success, except the curse damages the target at the end of each of its turns and lasts until the target spends a complete round without intentionally damaging another creature.
**Critical Failure** As success, except the curse damages the target at the end of each of its turns and lasts until the target spends 24 hours atoning for its perceived sins. --- **Heightened (+2)** The amount of mental damage the target takes for each creature it damaged that turn increases by 1d8, and the maximum damage the target can take per turn increases by 4d8.
name: Warrior's Regret

<title level="1" right="Cantrip 1" pfs="Standard">
[Wash Your Luck](/Spells.aspx?ID=1031)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Cantrip" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=22" />
<trait label="Fortune" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=76" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 140

**Traditions**
[Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Some practitioners argue this spell literally washes your fortunes of all influences, malign or benign, while others claim it simply cleanses your mind of obsessions about luck and destiny. You adjust your garments, change the posture of your chair, fidget with a religious symbol, or make some other innocuous and personally significant action to wash away bad luck.

 Once during the spell's duration, before rolling a check, you can cancel out a [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=110) effect on that roll, as normal when a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) and misfortune effect apply to the same roll. After canceling out the misfortune effect, the spell ends, and you are then temporarily immune to _wash your luck_ for 10 minutes.
name: Wash Your Luck

markdown:
<title level="1" right="Spell 3" pfs="Standard">
[Web of Eyes](/Spells.aspx?ID=1032)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=141" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 140

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Target** up to 5 willing creatures
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You place an [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=26) scrying sensor on each target just above their eyes. Each sensor looks where that target looks, and all the targets can link their vision briefly to help notice things one target sees but the others might not. Each target can use an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to share what it sees with any number of other targets until the start of its next turn. Only one creature can share its vision at a time, so if another target takes this action, the effect ends for any target that was previously sharing its vision.

 This improves how well the recipients can perceive anything the sharing creature is looking at. For instance, if a creature is [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=39) to a recipient but [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=27) by the sharing creature, the creature becomes observed by the recipient as well. Typically, the creature is seen as a glowing outline superimposed on its position. This might allow the recipient to target a creature it couldn't otherwise; however, cover and line of effect still might prevent or impede targeting and attacks. This can only improve the recipient's vision, not reduce it; for example, if an enemy was undetected by the sharing creature and observed by a recipient, the recipient would still clearly observe the enemy.

 Once the vision sharing stops, the benefit ends. Whether a creature is [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) or undetected is still based on the last information a target had before the vision sharing ended. For example, that means if a creature is behind a wall but hasn't moved, it's still hidden rather than undetected by a recipient that witnessed its current position.
name: Web of Eyes

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 141 **Tradition** [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 500 feet **Area** 15-foot radius, 80-foot tall cylinder **Saving Throw** Reflex **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- Powerful winds coalesce into a devastating tornado. You can Cast this Spell only if you are outside or the ceiling is 80 feet or higher. All squares in the whirlwind are difficult terrain. All creatures in the area take 5d10 bludgeoning damage as powerful winds and debris buffet them, with a Reflex save. Each time you Sustain the Spell, you can move the whirlwind up to 30 feet in a straight line. Each creature the whirlwind moves through takes the damage, also with a Reflex save. A creature can be affected by a _whirlwind_ only once per round. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and rises 10 feet into the air. If it doesn't have a fly Speed, the creature gains one equal to its Speed until it either reaches the ground or ceases to be in the whirlwind's area, potentially falling when the spell ends or when it leaves the area.
**Critical Failure** As failure, except the creature takes double damage and rises 20 feet into the air. --- **Heightened (+1)** Increase the bludgeoning damage by 1d10.
name: Whirlwind

<title level="1" right="Spell 4" pfs="Standard">
[Winning Streak](/Spells.aspx?ID=1034)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 141

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Marishi](/Deities.aspx?ID=650), [The Divine Dare](/Deities.aspx?ID=269)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 20 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 round (see text)
</row>

</column>

---

The target is energized by its good fortune as it spreads to others—as long as they keep winning. It gains the [quickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=32) condition for 1 round. If the target or one of their allies within 20 feet gets a critical success on an attack roll against a significant foe, whoever got the critical success becomes quickened if they weren't already, and the duration of the _winning streak_ is extended by another round. Creatures quickened by the spell can use the extra action to Strike, Step, or Stride. If a full round passes without the target or one their allies critically succeeding on an attack roll against a significant foe, the _winning streak_ breaks and the spell ends.
name: Winning Streak

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Standard">
[Zero Gravity](/Spells.aspx?ID=1035)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 141

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot radius, 40-foot tall cylinder

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You negate gravity's effects in the area. Creatures float in place unless they can Push Off a surface. Pushing Off is a single action which has the [move](/Traits.aspx?ID=114) trait, allowing the creature to move half its Speed in a straight line through the area. After Pushing Off a surface, the creature continues to move the same distance at the start of each of its turns until it leaves the area or Pushes Off against something else. A creature pushing against an untethered object of its size or smaller causes both the creature and the object to move at the same speed, but in opposite directions. Creatures who can levitate or fly can use those abilities to move around in the area without having to push off anything. Creatures who can't levitate or fly are usually on the ground, giving them a surface from which to push off.

 You can Dismiss this spell. When _zero gravity_ ends, all creatures and objects in the area fall back down.
name: Zero Gravity

You briefly turn your attack into pure force to bypass your opponent's defenses. Choose a target within your reach, or within the reach of a melee weapon you're wielding. If you can use [Spellstrike](/Actions.aspx?ID=755) with a ranged weapon (with the [starlit span](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=4) hybrid study, for example), you can target a creature in the first range increment of your ranged weapon. For an instant, you transform your weapon or unarmed attack into a spike of pure force, replacing all its normal statistics and abilities. The _force fang_ automatically deals 1d4+1 force damage to the target.

---
**Heightened (+2)** The damage increases by 1d4+1.

<actions string="Single Action" />

<traits>

<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

Your unarmed attacks or weapon gain the benefits of a weapon rune you choose when you cast this spell: [_corrosive_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=292), [_flaming_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=295), [_frost_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=296), [_ghost touch_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=297), [_returning_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=301), [_shock_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=303), or [_thundering_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=306). If you cast _runic impression_ on a weapon, this spell ends if you cease holding the weapon except if you throw a returning weapon.

 If this spell would give a weapon more property runes than its normal maximum, one of the existing property runes (you choose) is suppressed until the spell ends. For [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks, if this spell would give you more property runes than you could have from [_handwraps of mighty blows_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=441), one of the existing property runes is similarly suppressed.

---
**Heightened (8th)** Add [_keen_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=300) to the list of runes you can choose as well as the greater types of _corrosive_, _flaming_, _frost_, _shock_, and _thundering_.
name: Runic Impression

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 144 **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 100 feet **Target** your eidolon **Duration** 1 minute --- You flood your eidolon with power, creating a temporary evolution in your eidolon's capabilities. Choose one of the following effects:
  • Your eidolon gains [low-light vision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=23) and [darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12).
  • Your eidolon gains [scent](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=33) as an [imprecise sense](/Rules.aspx?ID=412) up to 30 feet.
  • Your eidolon can breathe underwater and gains a swim Speed equal to its land Speed or 30 feet, whichever is less. Alternatively, if your eidolon is normally aquatic, it can breathe air and gains a land Speed equal to its swim Speed or 30 feet, whichever is less. Either way, it gains the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=207) trait.
  • Your eidolon gains a +20-foot status bonus to its Speed.
--- **Heightened (3rd)** Add the following options to the list of effects you can choose:
  • Your eidolon becomes Large, instead of its previous size. This increases your eidolon's [reach](/Traits.aspx?ID=192) to 10 feet but doesn't change any other statistics for your eidolon. Because of the special link you share, you can ride your eidolon without getting in each other's way. If another creature tries to ride your eidolon, both you and the riding creature each regain only 2 actions at the start of your turns each round, as normal.
  • Your eidolon gains a climb Speed equal to its land Speed.
**Heightened (5th)** Add the options from the 3rd-level version and the following options to the list of effects you can choose:
  • Your eidolon becomes Huge, instead of its previous size. This has the same effects as the 3rd-level option to become Large, except your eidolon's reach increases to 15 feet.
  • Your eidolon gains a fly Speed equal to its Speed.
name: Evolution Surge

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 145 **Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) --- You focus on the intricacies of the magic binding you to your eidolon to extend the duration of your [_boost eidolon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1045) or [_reinforce eidolon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1050) spell. If your next action is to cast _boost eidolon_ or _reinforce eidolon_, attempt a skill check with the skill associated with the tradition of magic you gain from your eidolon (such as [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) for a [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) eidolon). The DC is usually a [standard-difficulty DC](/Rules.aspx?ID=554) of your level, but the GM can assign a different DC based on the circumstances. The effect depends on the result of your check. **Critical Success** The spell lasts 4 rounds.
**Success** The spell lasts 3 rounds.
**Failure** The spell lasts 1 round, but you don't spend the Focus Point for casting this spell.
name: Extend Boost

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1309)** **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 200, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61 **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 120 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Fortitude --- You ignite a creature in lasting flames. The fire deals 4d8 fire damage and 2d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the creature, which must attempt a Fortitude save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and takes no persistent damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage, as well as full persistent damage.
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, as well as double persistent damage. --- **Heightened (+1)** Increase the initial damage by 1d8 and the persistent damage by 1d6.
name: Combustion

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1306)** **Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 200, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61 **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 60 feet **Target** 1 creature **Saving Throw** Reflex **Duration** 1 round --- You tear open the ground then slam it shut. The target creature takes 2d6 bludgeoning damage with a Reflex save. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target takes half damage.
**Failure** The target takes full damage, is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58), and takes a –10-foot circumstance penalty to Speed.
**Critical Failure** The target takes double damage and is off-guard and [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24). It can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) against your spell DC. If it doesn't Escape, the target takes an additional 2d6 bludgeoning damage when the spell ends. --- **Heightened (+1)** Increase the initial damage and additional damage by 2d6.
name: Crushing Ground

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Powerful Inhalation](/Spells.aspx?ID=1054)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1305)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Area** 10-foot emanation

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude

</row>

</column>

---

You rapidly draw the air from your surroundings, hoarding it for yourself. Creatures in the area when the spell is cast take 5d6 bludgeoning damage with a basic Fortitude save. A creature that fails its save can't speak above a raspy whisper for 1 round. A creature that critically fails can't speak or use abilities that require it to breathe for 1 round. This prevents it from using effects that require speech, such as casting spells with verbal components, and from using a breath weapon or similar ability.

 Creatures made of air (such as [air elementals](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=41)) attempting a save against this spell get a degree of success one worse than they rolled.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the damage by 2d6.
name: Powerful Inhalation

[Pulverizing Cascade](/Spells.aspx?ID=1055)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1311)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 10-foot radius, 20-foot-tall cylinder

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

You raise a pair of towering waves and slam them into each other, crushing creatures caught between them. Creatures in the area take 5d6 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the damage by 2d6.
name: Pulverizing Cascade

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Rising Surf](/Spells.aspx?ID=1056)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Move" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=114" />
<trait label="Water" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=165" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1310)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You create a wave of water that you ride, banking around obstacles and surfing to higher ground. You move up to 35 feet, raising yourself up to 5 feet above the ground. (You can avoid many types of difficult terrain in this way.) You must end your movement on an unoccupied space where you have solid footing. This movement isn't a Stride, but you measure the distance in a similar way, and it still triggers reactions caused by movement. You can't transport anyone else with you.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Increase the distance you move by 5 feet and the maximum height traveled above the ground by 5 feet.
name: Rising Surf

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Stone Lance](/Spells.aspx?ID=1057)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Attack" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=15" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Earth" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=55" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1307)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You conjure a jagged lance of stone and then launch it at a foe. Make a spell attack roll against the target. On a hit, you deal 6d6 piercing damage and the lance impales the creature, giving it a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds unless it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=79). On a critical hit, if the creature is on the ground, the lance also embeds into the ground and [immobilizes](/Conditions.aspx?ID=24) the creature until it Escapes. A creature that Escapes after being impaled takes 3 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). When the spell ends, the lance crumbles into dirt, freeing the target if it hasn't Escaped.

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the damage by 2d6 and the persistent bleed damage for Escapes by 1.
name: Stone Lance

[Updraft](/Spells.aspx?ID=1058)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Air" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=5" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1304)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 60

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
[basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Reflex

</row>

</column>

---

A powerful blast of wind erupts from the ground, launching the target into the air and sending it crashing back down. The collision deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage with a basic Reflex save. On a failure, the target is knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31).

---
**Heightened (+1)** Increase the damage by 2d6.
name: Updraft

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Wildfire](/Spells.aspx?ID=1059)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Druid" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=52" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**There is a more recent version of this, [click here to view.](/Spells.aspx?ID=1308)**

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 201, [Rage of Elements](/Sources.aspx?ID=205) pg. 61

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

**Area** 10-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Reflex

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

A thin layer of ash and flame covers the ground in the area. The area becomes hazardous terrain. A creature that moves on the ground through the area takes 1 fire damage for every square of that area it moves into. A creature that ends its turn in the area must succeed at a Reflex save or take 1 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

 Each time you sustain this spell, the radius of the burst increases by 5 feet.

---
**Heightened (+2)** Increase the damage and persistent damage by 1.
name: Wildfire

<title level="1" right="Focus 7" pfs="Standard">
[Darklight](/Spells.aspx?ID=1060)
<actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Area** 20-foot burst

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

Shadows pour forth from your eyes, creating a field of [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409) that prevents light from emanating within the area. Light doesn't enter the area; any non-magical light sources, such as a [torches](/Equipment.aspx?ID=60) or [lanterns](/Equipment.aspx?ID=29), don't emanate any light while inside the area, even if their light radius would extend beyond the darkness. The spell similarly suppresses magical [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=100) of the spell's level or lower.

 Light can't pass through, so creatures in the area can't see outside. Even creatures with [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) (but not greater darkvision) can barely see through the darkness; any creatures seen through the darkness are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to them. Creatures with greater darkvision can see through the darkness normally.

 Creatures who move into the field or start their turn within the field have some of the life and light sucked out of them, taking 2d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 negative damage, with a [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save. 

When you cast this spell, choose up to four allies in the area to spare from _darklight's_ effects. Until the spell ends, they're immune to damage from _darklight_ spells and can see through _darklight_ (but not other darkness) as though they had greater darkvision.

---
**Heightened (10th)** The bludgeoning and negative damage both increase to 3d6.
name: Darklight

<title level="1" right="Focus 3" pfs="Standard">
[Inscrutable Mask](/Spells.aspx?ID=1061)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 227

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You shroud your form and features in an impenetrable mask of shadow. The mask grants you a +1 status bonus to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) or [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48), [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7) checks to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=53), and [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in areas of [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=408) or [darkness](/Rules.aspx?ID=409).

---
**Heightened (6th)** The status bonus increases to +2.

**Heightened (9th)** The status bonus increases to +3.
name: Inscrutable Mask

<title level="1" right="Focus 1" pfs="Standard">
[Efficient Apport](/Spells.aspx?ID=1062)
<actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Focus" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=511" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
<trait label="Wizard" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=166" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 241

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 60 feet

**Target** 1 unattended object of light Bulk or less
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

Walking over to an item to pick it up is so much effort. Whether it's your spellbook, a reagent, or a glass of wine, it's simply more efficient to call it to your hand. You teleport the target into your open hand. If you don't have a hand free, it falls to the ground at your feet.

---
**Heightened (3rd)** You can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less

**Heightened (5th)** The range increases to 120 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 1 or less.

**Heightened (7th)** The range increases to 120 feet, and you can target an unattended object with a Bulk of 2 or less.
name: Efficient Apport

<title level="1" right="Spell 6" pfs="Limited">
[Catch Your Name](/Spells.aspx?ID=1063)
<actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 247

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283)

 

**Trigger** A creature on your plane speaks your private or true name

**Requirements** You know your true name.
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Saving Throw** 
Will

</row>

</column>

---

You learn a private name (but not the true name) of the creature who spoke your name, as well as their precise location, unless they succeed at a Will save to negate the effect. You are aware any time the trigger conditions are met as long as you have the spell prepared or in your repertoire. There is no way to distinguish one trigger of this spell from another without actually casting the spell. This can become a nuisance if you are so famous that your name is regularly on others' lips.
name: Catch Your Name

markdown:
**Source** [Secrets of Magic](/Sources.aspx?ID=96) pg. 247 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** 1 creature whose true name you know **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** varies --- You instruct the target to do something, compelling obedience by calling it by its true name. Your instructions can't be self-destructive. Though this spell has the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) trait, this applies only to the instructions; the creature doesn't need to know its own true name to be affected. The target must attempt a Will saving throw. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected, is temporarily immune to further castings of this spell by you for 1 week, and learns your true name.
**Success** The target is unaffected, is temporarily immune to further castings of this spell by you for 1 day, and learns a fragment of your true name (see the Fragmentary Names sidebar).
**Failure** The target does as you command. The spell has a duration of 10 minutes, or until the target has completed a finite instruction or the instructions become self-destructive. The creature is temporarily immune to further castings of this spell by you for 1 hour.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the spell's duration is 8 hours, and the creature is not temporarily immune.
name: Compel True Name

<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

**Target** 1 willing creature with an  unarmed  claw Strike
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** sustained up to 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You sing a fierce prayer, low and growling, and awaken the ancient spirit of a Terwa beast to guide and strengthen the target's unarmed blows. The target's claw attack gains the [versatile (piercing)](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) trait, and deals 1d6 slashing damage. If the target's claw attack already deals 1d6 slashing damage, it deals 1d8 instead. If its claw attack already had the versatile (piercing) trait, it gains the [deadly 1d8](/Traits.aspx?ID=174) trait.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The spell can affect up to 6 willing creatures with unarmed claw Strikes.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 77 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Area** 10-foot burst **Saving Throw** basic Fortitude **Duration** sustained up to 1 minute --- With a rattling hiss, you create a quickly evaporating mist of invisible poisonous marsh gas to smother creatures in the area. When you Sustain the Spell, the mist dissipates completely from the previous area, and you can select a new area within range to fill with the mist. If you don't Sustain the Spell, the mist dissipates and the spell ends. You deal 4d6 poison damage to each breathing creature that starts its turn in the area. Each affected creature must attempt a Fortitude save. The creature is then temporarily immune to _gasping marsh_ for 1 minute. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, is sickened 2, and takes 1d8 [persistent poison damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
name: Gasping Marsh

May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #171: Hurricane's Howl](/Sources.aspx?ID=101) pg. 78 **Traditions** [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** 30 feet **Target** up to 4 creatures **Saving Throw** Fortitude **Duration** up to 1 minute --- You wrap the targeted creatures in thin, sickly green shrouds that sap information from attackers. The shroud settles about each target's shoulders, granting a +1 status bonus to AC and a +2 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks, both only while the target is in dim light or darkness. If a creature deals damage in melee to or touches a cloaked creature, the cloak wraps around the attacker, temporarily connecting the psyches of the cloaked creature and the attacker and violently prying a random piece of interesting information from the attacker's mind. The attacker takes 2d8 mental damage and must attempt a Will save. Afterward, the spell ends for the cloaked creature, and the attacker is temporarily immune to _mirecloak_ for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The attacker is unaffected.
**Success** The attacker takes half damage.
**Failure** The attacker takes full damage and is [sickened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34). In addition, the cloaked creature gleans a piece of information from the attacker's mind. The details and extent of this information are at the GM's discretion.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the attacker takes double damage and is sickened 2.
name: Mirecloak

If you need to determine whether the light passes through an area of darkness, draw a line between yourself and the spell's target. --- **Heightened (+1)** The cold damage increases by 2d6, and the good damage against fiends and undead increases by 2d6.

[Penumbral Disguise](/Spells.aspx?ID=1083)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Shadow" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=143" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 77

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deity**
[Dajermube](/Deities.aspx?ID=338)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** touch

**Target** 1 willing creature
</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 10 minutes
</row>

</column>

---

You wrap the target in shadows, granting them a +1 status bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) while in dim light or darkness. In addition, the shadows mask the creature's features. While the creature is in dim light or darkness, other creatures must succeed at a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84) action against the spell's DC to discern details about the target's appearance. For example, without using Seek, other creatures can determine the target's general shape (such as humanoid), but they would need to Seek to determine the target's precise appearance or any other identifying information. Creatures with [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) can still see the target and their features normally. The target's normal appearance is revealed in bright light.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The status bonus is +2. Creatures with darkvision can no longer discern details about the target while the target is in dim light or darkness without Seeking, though creatures with [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12) can still determine these details.

**Heightened (6th)** As 4th level, except the status bonus is +3 and creatures without darkvision can't determine even general details about the target while the target is in dim light or darkness unless they successfully Seek the target; these creatures see a vague shadow instead. Even on a successful Seek, they only determine general features, though they can see details on a critical success.

**Heightened (8th)** As 6th level, except the status bonus is +4 and even creatures with greater darkvision must Seek to discern details about the target while the target is in dim light or darkness.
name: Penumbral Disguise

You form a wall of brilliant light that obscures creatures. You create a thin wall of light in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. The wall stands vertically. If you wish, the wall can be of a shorter length or height. Creatures adjacent to the wall are [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=7). Creatures that move through the wall take 2d6 fire damage ([basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=329) Fortitude save) as the concentrated light burns them.

---
**Heightened (4th)** The bright light makes it difficult to discern creatures on the other side. Creatures are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to other creatures on the opposite side of the wall from them. The fire damage increases to 3d6.

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Darkness" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=39" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 78

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

**Deities**
[Aleth](/Deities.aspx?ID=328), [Children of the Night](/Deities.aspx?ID=376)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 30 feet

</row>

<row gap="medium">

**Duration** 1 minute
</row>

</column>

---

You form a wall of pure darkness in a straight line up to 60 feet long and 10 feet high. You must create the wall in an unbroken open space so its edges don't pass through any creatures or objects, or the spell is lost. The wall stands vertically and, if you wish, can be of a shorter length or height. The wall prevents light from passing through and appears as a sheet of pure darkness to creatures observing it. Creatures without [darkvision](/Rules.aspx?ID=415) or those unable to see through darkness can't see creatures on the other side of the wall. The wall is too thin for creatures to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62) in the darkness itself, but creatures can Hide from creatures on the other side of the wall as normal.

---
**Heightened (5th)** Creatures with darkvision (but not [greater darkvision](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=12)) can barely see through the wall. They treat targets seen through the wall as [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4).

**Heightened (7th)** Creatures with greater darkvision can barely see through the darkness. They treat targets seen through the wall as concealed. All other creatures are unable to see through the darkness at all.
name: Wall of Shadow

<title level="1" right="Spell 7" pfs="Limited">
[Return to Essence](/Spells.aspx?ID=1087)
<actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 77

**Traditions**
[Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3)

<row gap="medium">

**Cast** [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284)

 

</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Range** 120 feet

**Target** one magically sealed or warded doorway or chest
</row>

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

You unravel the magic of a spell that affects a container, door, or lock, converting it into energy you can repurpose to power your own spells. Attempt a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check against the target spell or magical effect that wards or seals a doorway or chest, such as [_lock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=174), or against a spell or effect that deals damage or delivers secondary effects when it's opened, such as [_glyph of warding_](/Spells.aspx?ID=138). If you succeed, you recover its energy, restoring one of your expended spell slots of 3rd level or lower.

---
**Heightened (8th)** You can restore an expended spell slot of 4th level or lower.

**Heightened (9th)** You can restore an expended spell slot of 5th level or lower.

**Heightened (10th)** You can restore an expended spell slot of 6th level or lower.
name: Return to Essence

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Strength of Thousands **Source** [Pathfinder #173: Doorway to the Red Star](/Sources.aspx?ID=103) pg. 78 **Traditions** [Arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) **Cast** [material](/Rules.aspx?ID=282), [somatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=283), [verbal](/Rules.aspx?ID=284) **Range** touch **Target** 1 container or doorway **Saving Throw** Will **Duration** 10 minutes --- You ward a target doorway or container with a temporal trap that rewinds the personal timeline of creatures trying to open it. A creature attempting to open a warded doorway or container must attempt a Will save with the below results. You can set a password for the ward. Any creature that attempts to open the target container, or to open or pass through the target doorway without speaking the password must attempt a save as normal. The ward vanishes automatically once the spell's duration ends, but can be removed before then as if it were a magical trap, using your spell DC for both the Perception check to notice it and [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to disable it. Both checks require the creature attempting them to have expert proficiency in order to succeed. You can Dismiss _temporal ward_. The maximum number of _temporal wards_ you can have active at a time is equal to your spellcasting ability modifier. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected and opens the doorway or the container as normal.
**Success** The creature experiences a brief moment of dé jà-vu in which they're convinced they already opened the door. They must use an additional action to open the door. If they can't or don't want to do so, the action or activity they used to open the door or container is wasted, and the door or container remains closed.
**Failure** The creature is thrown briefly backwards in time. The action or activity they used to open the door or container is wasted and they're returned to the position they were located at during the start of their turn. Their turn then ends.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the temporal displacement also causes the creature to lose their bearings, making them [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 minute. --- **Heightened (10th)** The _temporal ward_ has an unlimited duration, until it is [counteracted](/Rules.aspx?ID=371), Dismissed, or Disarmed.
name: Temporal Ward
